title: Best AI Project Management Tools for Side Projects in 2026
excerpt: Notion, Asana, Linear, Todoist, and Kanban AI compared for indie builders — with a focus on AI planning, MCP agent integration, and keeping side projects shippable without enterprise overhead.
author: Kanban AI Team
body:
# Best AI Project Management Tools for Side Projects in 2026

Side projects die in the planning phase. Not from lack of talent — from **tools built for enterprises** that turn a weekend app into a ceremony of workspaces, permissions, and integrations you will never finish configuring.

In 2026, the best PM tools for side projects share three traits:

1. **AI that breaks down work** — not just a chat box, but planning that produces actionable cards.
2. **Low ceremony** — open a board tonight, not after a two-hour setup ritual.
3. **Agent integration** — your Cursor or Claude session can read and update the same board you see.

We compared the tools indie builders actually reach for — and where **Kanban AI** fits when MCP and board-native AI planning matter.

---

## What side-project builders actually need

| Need | Why it matters |
|------|----------------|
| **Fast start** | You have 8 hours a week, not a procurement cycle |
| **Visible progress** | A board that shows you have been "almost done" for six weeks |
| **AI breakdown** | Turn "build auth" into tasks you can finish on a Tuesday night |
| **Agent access** | Let your coding agent update cards without copy-paste |
| **Affordable** | Free tier or low cost — side projects are not billable yet |

Enterprise PM tools optimize for cross-functional teams, OKRs, and compliance. Side projects need **shipping velocity**.

---

## The contenders

### Kanban AI — board + AI + MCP in one stack

**Best for:** Builders who want a kanban board, AI planning, and Cursor/Claude MCP without adopting a full issue tracker.

Kanban AI is purpose-built for the side-project loop: describe your idea, get an AI-generated plan with sprint-aware tasks, manage execution on a kanban board, and connect your coding agent via MCP at [/connect](https://kanbanai.dev/connect).

**Standout features:**
- **AI planning wizard** — paste a project description, get phased cards under two hours each
- **@kanban task comments** — AI replies in context on individual cards
- **MCP integration** — 11 tools so your agent lists projects, moves cards, and adds comments
- **Guest board** at [/kanban](https://kanbanai.dev/kanban) — no signup required to start
- **Open source (MIT)** — inspect, fork, or self-host from [GitHub](https://github.com/orholam/kanban_ai)

**Trade-off:** Not a docs wiki or email hub — it is a focused kanban + AI product, not a workspace platform.

---

### Notion — flexible workspace with AI writing

**Best for:** Builders who want docs, notes, and light task tracking in one place.

Notion's AI helps draft pages, summarize content, and fill databases. Templates for project trackers are abundant.

**Strengths:** Incredible flexibility. Great for project wikis and mixed content. AI writing is polished.

**Trade-offs:**
- No native MCP — your coding agent cannot directly update Notion tasks
- Kanban views are database views, not a board-first product
- AI planning produces pages, not sprint-aware cards with assignees and due dates
- Easy to spend more time designing the workspace than shipping code

---

### Asana — structured task management with AI summaries

**Best for:** Side projects that grow into small teams needing assignments and timelines.

Asana added AI features for status summaries, goal suggestions, and smart fields. Board and list views are mature.

**Strengths:** Clean task model. Good mobile apps. AI helps with status updates and field suggestions.

**Trade-offs:**
- No MCP integration for coding agents
- Free tier limits collaborators and views
- AI assists existing tasks — it does not generate a full project breakdown from a description
- Feels enterprise-leaning even on small projects

---

### Linear — issue tracker for serious side projects

**Best for:** Side projects that are clearly software products with bugs, cycles, and Git integrations.

Linear has official MCP support and a polished agent ecosystem. AI features assist triage and drafting.

**Strengths:** Best issue tracking UX in this list. Official remote MCP server. Cycles and roadmaps when you need them.

**Trade-offs:**
- Issue tracker, not kanban-first — boards are a secondary view
- Heavier model than "four columns and ship" for a weekend tool
- AI planning is separate from a dedicated board-native wizard
- Overkill if your side project is a script, a course, or a non-software build

---

### Todoist — personal task lists with AI task creation

**Best for:** Solo builders who want a fast inbox, not a visual board.

Todoist's AI helps parse natural language into tasks and suggests due dates. Ramble feature turns voice notes into tasks.

**Strengths:** Fastest capture of any tool here. Great mobile experience. AI task parsing is genuinely useful.

**Trade-offs:**
- List-based, not kanban — no columns, no WIP limits, no sprint view
- No MCP for coding agents
- No project-level AI planning — individual task creation, not phased roadmaps
- Hard to see the shape of a multi-week build

---

### Trello — classic kanban, AI via Atlassian Intelligence

**Best for:** Builders who want the simplest possible board and already use the Atlassian ecosystem.

Trello remains the default "just a board" tool. Atlassian Intelligence adds AI-powered card descriptions and summaries.

**Strengths:** Everyone knows how to use it. Power-Ups extend functionality. Zero learning curve.

**Trade-offs:**
- No MCP integration for coding agents
- AI is card-level assistance, not project-level planning
- Atlassian account requirements for newer AI features
- Stagnant core product — innovation lives in Power-Ups and paid tiers

---

## Comparison table

| Tool | AI planning | MCP for agents | Board-first | Free tier | Side-project fit |
|------|-------------|----------------|-------------|-----------|------------------|
| **Kanban AI** | ✅ Project wizard | ✅ Native remote | ✅ Yes | ✅ Guest + free tier | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ |
| Notion | ⚠️ Page writing | ❌ | ❌ Database view | ✅ Limited | ⭐⭐⭐ |
| Asana | ⚠️ Task assist | ❌ | ⚠️ Board view | ⚠️ Limited | ⭐⭐⭐ |
| Linear | ⚠️ Triage assist | ✅ Official | ❌ Issue tracker | ✅ Limited | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ |
| Todoist | ⚠️ Task parsing | ❌ | ❌ List | ✅ Generous | ⭐⭐⭐ |
| Trello | ⚠️ Card assist | ❌ | ✅ Yes | ✅ | ⭐⭐⭐ |

*"AI planning" = generating a phased project breakdown from a description, not just rewriting a single card.*

---

## The MCP advantage for side projects

If you code in Cursor or Claude Desktop, **MCP changes the side-project loop**:

| Without MCP | With MCP (Kanban AI) |
|-------------|----------------------|
| Copy task titles into your editor manually | Agent reads the board and picks up the next card |
| Switch to browser to mark tasks done | Agent moves cards to Done after implementing |
| Lose context between chat and board | Agent comments on cards with implementation notes |
| Planning lives in chat history | Planning lives on the board your agent shares |

Only **Kanban AI** and **Linear** in this list offer native MCP. Linear's MCP is issue-tracker-centric. Kanban AI's MCP maps directly to kanban board actions — the same cards you drag in the browser.

---

## Our recommendation

| Your side project looks like… | Start here |
|-------------------------------|------------|
| Software you build in Cursor/Claude | **Kanban AI** — AI plan + board + MCP |
| Docs-heavy project (course, book, research) | Notion |
| Growing into a small team with deadlines | Asana |
| Serious SaaS with issues and cycles | Linear |
| Quick personal todos, no board needed | Todoist |
| Simplest possible visual board | Trello |

For most indie builders shipping code on nights and weekends, the winning combo is **AI that writes the plan** + **a board that shows progress** + **an agent that updates both**. That is what we optimized Kanban AI for.

---

## Getting started with Kanban AI

1. Open the guest board at [/kanban](https://kanbanai.dev/kanban) — no account needed.
2. Describe your side project and let the AI planning wizard generate cards.
3. Sign in when you want cloud sync and team features.
4. Connect your agent at [/connect](https://kanbanai.dev/connect) so Cursor or Claude manages the same board.

Example agent prompt once connected:

> "List my side project boards, show in-progress tasks in the current sprint, and add a comment on the auth card noting the JWT middleware is done."

---

## FAQ

### Do I need a PM tool for a side project at all?

Not always — a README checklist works for tiny builds. But if your project spans more than two weeks or more than ten tasks, a board prevents the "almost done" trap. The tool should take five minutes to set up, not become a second project.

### Is Notion good enough for software side projects?

Notion excels when your project is **content-heavy** — docs, specs, research, mixed media. For code-heavy builds where you want sprint-aware tasks, assignees, and agent integration, a board-first tool with MCP is a better fit.

### Why does MCP matter if I already use AI chat?

Chat is ephemeral. Your agent's plan disappears in conversation history. MCP lets the agent **read and write a persistent board** — so Monday night's plan is still there Wednesday, and your agent can pick up where it left off without you re-explaining context.

### Is Linear overkill for a side project?

For a weekend script, yes. For a side project SaaS with bugs, releases, and Git-linked issues, Linear is excellent — especially with its MCP server. The question is whether you need an issue tracker or a kanban board. Most early-stage side projects need the latter.

### Can I use Kanban AI without signing up?

Yes. The guest board at [/kanban](https://kanbanai.dev/kanban) works without an account. Sign in when you want cloud sync, team members, and MCP agent access via [/connect](https://kanbanai.dev/connect).

### How is this different from your summer side-project playbook?

Our [summer shipping playbook](/blog/finish-side-project-summer-ai-kanban) is a **how-to** — weekly rituals, column setup, and habits. This post is a **tool comparison** to help you pick the right software before you start.

New to the category? Read the [AI kanban board guide](/blog/ai-kanban-board-guide-2026) for definitions and a buyer checklist before you compare vendors.

---
